original_prompt: Please show me the IDs of the files in the cloud drive that are shared with 'alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com'.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt provides the key selector (the email) and the desired output (file IDs), so a competent agent would correctly choose a file-listing first tool (e.g., list_files) and then filter by shared-with metadata. However it omits several operational details that may affect execution: which cloud drive/account/context to use, whether “shared with” includes groups or link-based sharing, whether folders should be included, and output/formatting preferences. Thus the first-tool path is preserved but meaningful uncertainties remain.

exposure_request: Please show me the IDs of the files in the cloud drive that are shared with 'alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com'.

natural_level_plan.rationale: The original request already contains the single strong trigger needed to choose the same first tool (list_files): the user asks for file IDs in the cloud drive that are shared with a specific email. There are only a few meaningful, natural axes of ambiguity (verbosity/politeness vs terse minimal form) available without introducing new clarifiers that were not in the original prompt. Therefore two distinct, useful levels (a terse/minimal phrasing and the original phrasing) capture the natural ambiguity range while preserving the same first-tool path and all task-critical references.

levels.description: Most ambiguous / terse: minimal phrasing that preserves the core request but omits any extra wording or disambiguation.
Original / less ambiguous: the full original sentence (polite request) — near the most execution-ready form possible without adding new constraints.
must_include: Please show me the IDs of the files in the cloud drive that are shared with 'alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com'.

natural_levels.N1: Please show me the IDs of the files in the cloud drive that are shared with 'alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com'.
natural_levels.N2: Please list the IDs of all files in the cloud drive that are shared with the user 'alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com'.

N1.reasoning: The prompt clearly identifies the object to find (files in the cloud drive) and the filter (shared with alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com), so a competent agent would pick a file-listing call (e.g., list_files) as the first tool. However it omits several operational specifics that could matter later (which drive/account if multiple, whether link-shared files count, permission types to consider, folder/scope constraints, desired output format beyond IDs). These omissions don’t change the correct first tool family but leave non-trivial follow-up details open.

N2.reasoning: The prompt clearly identifies the object (cloud drive files), the filter (shared with alex.martin@bluesparrowtech.com) and the desired output (file IDs), so the correct first tool path (list_files) is preserved. However it omits several operational details that could affect execution (e.g., whether to include files shared via links or groups, whether to include trashed files or folders, expected output format, deduplication, pagination), so while workable the request is still quite underspecified for full automation.
